meta.note: Weekend pre-market briefing. Articles sourced via web_search + web_extract deep extraction.

articles.title: Markets News, June 12, 2026: Indexes Close Higher on SpaceX Debut Day, Finish Up for Week; Oil Falls on Hopes of US-Iran Deal
articles.summary: Dow +0.7%, S&P 500 +0.5%, Nasdaq +0.3%. S&P 500 marks 10th positive week in 11. SpaceX IPO closed at $160.95 (+19%). Oil fell on Iran deal hopes: WTI -3.2% to $84.88, Brent -3.4% to $87.33. PPI +1.1% MoM, +6.5% YoY — highest since Nov 2022. Jobless claims 229k, highest since Feb. Stagflation warning.

articles.title: Stock market next week: Outlook for June 15-19, 2026
articles.summary: SpaceX IPO aftermath and Nasdaq-100 inclusion in 15 trading days. Semiconductors ETF SOXX up >11% for week. Russell 2000 hit all-time highs, +4% week, +19% YTD. FOMC June 16-17: Warsh's first meeting, rates expected unchanged, easing bias removal likely. Week ahead calendar: Empire State, Industrial Production, Retail Sales, FOMC decision. Markets closed Friday June 19 for Juneteenth.

articles.title: For Warsh as Fed chair, silence may be the point
articles.summary: Warsh plans 'regime change' in Fed communications: fewer press conferences (4/year vs 8), less forward guidance, possible dot-plot reform. Wants to eliminate easing bias. Three FOMC members dissented last meeting to remove it. Markets have 'almost no idea' where Warsh stands on rates. J.P. Morgan: Warsh 'could see him saying he can't rule out' rate hikes.

articles.title: Fed Signals Shift At June Meeting With Markets Pricing In 2026 Hike
articles.summary: Gov. Waller supports removing easing bias. CPI headline 3.8%, core 2.8% — both above 2% target. Markets pricing 1-2 rate hikes in 2026. Rate cuts off the table. Warsh may decline to discuss future policy in detail at first meeting.

articles.title: Brent falls to lowest since March on expected peace deal
articles.summary: Brent $87.33 (-3.4%), lowest since March. WTI $84.88 (-3.2%). U.S.-Iran MoU could be signed as soon as Sunday in Geneva. Iran's Araghchi says MoU 'has never been closer.' Trump called off airstrikes Thursday. ING: $120-130/bbl risk if flows don't resume by late July. Goldman cut 2027 Brent forecast to $80/bbl. OPEC cut 2026 demand growth to 970k bpd.

articles.title: Dollar forecast - Forex Friday | June 12, 2026
articles.summary: Dollar Index bearish engulfing candle at 100.00 resistance. Iran FM: MoU 'never been closer.' SpaceX IPO creating dollar demand. Week ahead: BoJ (Tue, 25bp hike priced in), FOMC (Wed, hawkish tone expected), BoE (Thu). Dollar direction hinges on FOMC forward guidance.

articles.title: While BTC price holds near $63,000, some data points to pain ahead for bulls
articles.summary: BTC $63,702, only 9% above realized price of ~$53,600. ETF demand shrinking at fastest pace since launch. Total BTC demand fell 652k BTC last week — largest contraction since Jan 2022. Sellers crystallized 187k BTC losses over 30 days. Options market biased for bounce to $75,000 by late July. VELVET surged 1,400% on pre-IPO hype, faces sell-the-news risk.

articles.title: Gold's record rally falters as bulls run into Fed rate expectations, stronger dollar
articles.summary: Gold at $4,187/oz, down 25% from January record of $5,595. Rally faltering on Fed rate expectations and stronger dollar. Speculative mania overboughtness reached 43.4% above 200-day moving average at peak.

articles.title: SpaceX (SPCX) IPO: Live updates — SPCX closes at $161, jumping 19%
articles.summary: SPCX: IPO $135, opened $150, closed $160.95 (+19.2%). After-hours $166.76. Market cap ~$2.1T at close, ~$2.2T after-hours. $75B raised — largest IPO ever. 500M+ shares traded. CFRA: Sell, $115 PT. NYU Damodaran: richly priced, bull case $1.3T. Starlink only profitable segment; SpaceX lost $4.9B in 2025. xAI acquisition included. Space stocks sold off: Virgin Galactic -34%, Rocket Lab -10%+.

articles.title: AAII Sentiment Survey: Pessimism Surges
articles.summary: AAII Bears: 47.7%, Bulls: 30.3%, Neutral: 22.0%. Bull-Bear spread -17.29%, surging from -0.73% prior week. Near extreme pessimism territory — historically a contrarian bullish signal.

articles.title: Weekly Trader's Stock Market Outlook
articles.summary: Stocks on track for weekly gains following bumpy start. Reports of potential Iran deal, lower oil prices and yields lift investor sentiment.

articles.title: Economic Data This Week (June 15-19): Retail Sales, FOMC, Industrial Production
articles.summary: Week ahead: Monday — Empire State, Industrial Production, Capacity Utilization. Tuesday — Housing Starts, Import/Export Prices. Wednesday — Retail Sales (May), FOMC decision + projections, Jabil earnings. Thursday — Jobless Claims, Philly Fed, Leading Indicators, Accenture & Kroger earnings. Friday — Markets closed (Juneteenth).
